raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Existing California law requires health plans to maintain accurate and up-to-date directories of in-network healthcare providers.",
    "Inadequate enforcement of the existing law has led to widespread inaccuracies in health plan provider directories.",
    "Consumers frequently encounter providers who are unreachable, unavailable, or no longer accepting patients due to inaccuracies in provider directories.",
    "The Department of Managed Health Care (DMHC) has taken only five enforcement actions against health plans since 2016.",
    "One health plan was found to have an error rate of 80 percent for psychiatrists.",
    "Error rates are high among directories for mental health providers and primary and specialty care providers.",
    "Annual verifications of provider information will be required under Assembly Bill 280.",
    "Health plans will be required to meet accuracy benchmarks starting at 60 percent and reaching 95 percent by 2029.",
    "Penalties will be imposed on health plans that fail to meet the established accuracy standards.",
    "Health plan provider directories are crucial for consumers to find in-network care.",
    "Inaccurate provider directories can lead to delays in needed care, worsening existing health conditions.",
    "AB 280 aims to improve transparency and accountability in health plan provider directories."
  ]
}
```
